question.text: Unrealized gains/losses on open commodity derivative positions at the end of an accounting period:

answer.text: Must be recognized in the books based on mark-to-market valuation
Can be ignored until the position is closed
Are only recognized if the position is profitable
Are recognized only for audit purposes

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Unrealized P&L on open positions must be recognized based on MTM at the period-end settlement price. This follows the prudence principle of accounting. **The Trap:** Can't be ignored. Must be recognized regardless of profit/loss direction. Required for all accounting purposes, not just audit. **Cross-Exam Context:** Period-end valuation of derivatives is a standard accounting requirement. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Period-end MTM can create tax implications — even if you haven't closed the position, the unrealized gain may be taxable as business income.
